title: To My Wife With A Copy Of My Poems
content:
I can write no stately proem
    As a prelude to my lay;
    From a poet to a poem
    I would dare to say.

    For if of these fallen petals
    One to you seem fair,
    Love will waft it till it settles
    On your hair.

    And when wind and winter harden
    All the loveless land,
    It will whisper of the garden,
    You will understand.
meaning:
Oscar Fingal O'Flahertie Wills Wilde's contribution to classic is further solidified by the brilliance found in "To My Wife With A Copy Of My Poems"...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
